children.text: monitor the performance by each accrediting entity of its duties under and its compliance with the requirements of the Convention, this chapter, other applicable laws, and implementing regulations under this chapter; andsection 14922 of this title

suspend or cancel the designation of an accrediting entity found to be substantially out of compliance with the Convention, this chapter, other applicable laws, or implementing regulations under this chapter.

children.heading: Suspension or cancellation of accreditation or approval
children.label: b Suspension or cancellation of accreditation or approval
A
children.text: the agency or person is substantially out of compliance with applicable requirements; and

children.label: B
children.text: the accrediting entity has failed or refused, after consultation with the Secretary, to take appropriate enforcement action.

children.label: A
children.text: notify the accrediting entity that the deficiencies have been corrected; and

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a suspension, terminate the suspension; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a cancellation, notify the agency or person that the agency or person may re-apply to the accrediting entity for accreditation or approval.

children.label: A
children.text: there is substantial evidence that the agency or person is out of compliance with applicable requirements; and

children.label: B
children.text: there has been a pattern of serious, willful, or grossly negligent failures to comply or other aggravating circumstances indicating that continued accreditation or approval would not be in the best interests of the children and families concerned.

children.heading: Period of debarment
children.label: 2 Period of debarment
children.text: The Secretary’s debarment order shall state whether the debarment is temporary or permanent. If the debarment is temporary, the Secretary shall specify a date, not earlier than 3 years after the date of the order, on or after which the agency or person may apply to the Secretary for withdrawal of the debarment.

children.heading: Effect of debarment
children.label: 3 Effect of debarment
children.text: An accrediting entity may take into account the circumstances of the debarment of an agency or person that has been debarred pursuant to this subsection in considering any subsequent application of the agency or person, or of any other entity in which the agency or person has an ownership or control interest, for accreditation or approval under this subchapter.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: d Judicial review
children.text: A person (other than a prospective adoptive parent), an agency, or an accrediting entity who is the subject of a final action of suspension, cancellation, or debarment by the Secretary under this subchapter may petition the United States District Court for the District of Columbia or the United States district court in the judicial district in which the person resides or the agency or accrediting entity is located to set aside the action. The court shall review the action in accordance with .section 706 of title 5

children.heading: Failure to ensure a full and complete home study
children.label: e Failure to ensure a full and complete home study

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Willful, grossly negligent, or repeated failure to ensure the completion and transmission of a background report (home study) that fully complies with the requirements of shall constitute substantial noncompliance with applicable requirements.section 14923(b)(1)(A)(ii) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: frequent and careful monitoring of compliance by agencies and approved persons with the requirements of section 14923(b)(A)(ii) of this title; and1So in original. Probably should be section “14923(b)(1)(A)(ii)”.1

children.label: B
children.text: consultation between the Secretary and the accrediting entity where an agency or person has engaged in substantial noncompliance with the requirements of section 14923(b)(A)(ii) of this title, unless the accrediting entity has taken appropriate corrective action and the noncompliance has not recurred.1

children.heading: Repeated failures to comply
children.label: 3 Repeated failures to comply
children.text: Repeated serious, willful, or grossly negligent failures to comply with the requirements of by an agency or person after consultation between Secretary and the accrediting entity with respect to previous noncompliance by such agency or person shall constitute a pattern of serious, willful, or grossly negligent failures to comply under subsection (c)(1)(B).section 14923(b)(1)(A)(ii) of this title

children.heading: Failure to comply with certain requirements
children.label: 4 Failure to comply with certain requirements
children.text: A failure to comply with the requirements of shall constitute a serious failure to comply under subsection (c)(1)(B) unless it is shown by clear and convincing evidence that such noncompliance had neither the purpose nor the effect of determining the outcome of a decision or proceeding by a court or other competent authority in the United States or the child’s country of origin.section 14923(b)(1)(A)(ii) of this title
